- content: "Meeting notes\nWe discussed the new product features and the marketing\
    \ strategy in detail. The meeting was held in the main\nconference room and was\
    \ attended by the heads of the Development, Marketing, and Product teams. The\
    \ primary\nfocus was on enhancing the user experience and incorporating feedback\
    \ received from the initial market tests.\n\nAction items:\n  - Follow up with\
    \ the marketing team to finalize the go-to-market strategy. This includes setting\
    \ a timeline for the\n    campaign launch and identifying the key marketing channels.\n\
    \n  - Schedule a meeting with the development team to discuss the technical feasibility\
    \ of the proposed features.\n    It is crucial to assess the development timeline\
    \ and resource allocation.\n\n  - Update the product roadmap to reflect the new\
    \ features and timelines discussed. This updated roadmap\n    will be shared with\
    \ all stakeholders for transparency and to ensure alignment.\n\n  - Prepare a\
    \ competitive analysis report by analyzing the current market trends and competitor\
    \ products.\n    This will help in positioning our product effectively.\n\n  -\
    \ Organize a focus group session with select users to gather direct feedback on\
    \ the prototype.\n    This feedback will be invaluable in making the final adjustments\
    \ before the launch.\n\n  - Develop a contingency plan to address potential risks\
    \ identified during the meeting. This\n    includes supply chain disruptions,\
    \ technology challenges, and market entry barriers.\n\nThe next steps involve\
    \ each team working on their assigned tasks and reconvening in two weeks\nfor\
    \ a progress update. The goal is to launch the product by the end of the quarter,\
    \ and every\neffort will be made to adhere to this timeline.\n"

I found two files titled meeting notes. The most relevant appears to be 2024-05-08_product-meeting.docx (owner: alex.martin@bluesparrowtech.com) which contains the action items you referenced. Before I email people, a few quick clarifying points so I send the right messages:

1) Which notes should I use?
   - 2024-05-08_product-meeting.docx (contains the action items you mentioned)
   - newest-client-meeting-minutes.docx (older, not clearly relevant)
   Confirm which file to use (I recommend the 2024-05-08 file).

2) Who exactly should I email?
   - From the file metadata I can immediately email: alex.martin@bluesparrowtech.com and david.lee@bluesparrowtech.com (the file is also shared with you).
   - The meeting text refers to “the heads of the Development, Marketing, and Product teams” but does not list their email addresses or names. Do you want me to:
     a) Email the file owner and the people it’s already shared with (alex.martin@..., david.lee@..., and you), or
     b) Email the team heads mentioned (Development, Marketing, Product)? If so, please provide their emails (or confirm their names so I can look them up), or
     c) Send a single group email to all stakeholders instead (if there is a team mailing list you want used, please provide it)?

3) One message or separate, tailored messages?
   - I can send one group email summarizing all action items, or
   - Send separate tailored emails to each person/team calling out only their tasks.
   Which do you prefer?

4) Subject, tone, and deadline
   - I can use a draft subject and ask recipients to confirm ownership and provide a status update within the two-week window mentioned in the notes. If you want a specific deadline date, tell me the date or I can compute "two weeks from today" (I can check the current day first if you want an exact date).

Suggested drafts (choose one option or edit them):

A) Group email (single message to all recipients)
Subject: Action items from product meeting (May 8) — next steps & ownership
Body:
Hi all,
Following up on the product meeting on May 8. Summary of action items and requested owners:
- Marketing: finalize the go-to-market strategy — set campaign launch timeline and identify key marketing channels.
- Development: schedule a meeting to review technical feasibility of proposed features and assess development timeline and resource needs.
- Product: update the product roadmap to reflect the new features and timelines; share the updated roadmap with stakeholders.
- (Unassigned) Prepare a competitive analysis report on current market trends and competitors — please confirm who will take this on.
- (Unassigned) Organize a focus group with selected users to gather prototype feedback — please confirm who will lead.
- (Unassigned) Develop a contingency plan for identified risks (supply chain, technology, market entry).
Please confirm ownership of the items above and provide a brief status/update within 2 weeks so we can reconvene for the progress check. If you have questions, reply or propose a time to meet.
Thanks,
Emma Johnson

B) Tailored examples (one per team head)
- Marketing subject: Finalize go-to-market strategy — action required
  Body: Hi [Marketing Head], please finalize the go-to-market strategy (timeline + channels) and confirm the launch timeline. Please reply with owner and timeline within 2 weeks.

- Development subject: Schedule feasibility meeting — action required
  Body: Hi [Dev Head], please schedule a meeting with Product to assess technical feasibility, timeline, and resource needs for the proposed features. Provide proposed meeting times and an initial feasibility assessment within 2 weeks.

- Product subject: Update product roadmap — action required
  Body: Hi [Product Head], please update the roadmap to include the new features and timelines discussed, and share the updated roadmap with stakeholders. Confirm when the updated roadmap will be available (within 2 weeks if possible).

If you confirm which file to use, who exactly to email (and whether to use group or tailored emails), and whether to use the suggested subjects/deadlines or provide alternatives, I will send the emails.
